title: Pseudophegopteris cruciata
intro: Pseudophegopteris cruciata is a species of fern in the family Matoniaceae, native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Pseudophegopteris cruciata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. In traditional medicine, Pseudophegopteris cruciata is commonly prepared as a tea or extract, and it is also used in combination with other herbs in traditional remedies for its potential health benefits.
